title: hyptis cruciformis
intro: Hyptis cruciformis, also known as Mexican tea or broadleaf hyptis, is a plant species that belongs to the Lamiaceae family and is native to Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. Its health benefits include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, and aiding in the treatment of wounds and skin conditions. The plant can be prepared into various herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
